revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:宝来山古墳]] ([[:ja:宝萊山古墳|宝萊山古墳]]) looks really promising and notable, and it looks pretty cool too. I think I will want to save it a bit since it is clearly notable and I might be able to if I get permission over it, use it as a proof of concept that I can reliably interpret Japanese, and potentially get restrictions lifted. But that would involve getting administrator approval to try it out. Like maybe I could get special permission to use Japanese sources just in that draft as a way to prove I could understand it. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:21, 14 November 2023 (UTC)}} . - [[User:Knowledgekid87|Knowledgekid87]] ([[User talk:Knowledgekid87|talk]]) 18:27, 14 November 2023 (UTC)}} so looks cool. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:30, 14 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Horai-yama Kofun, zenkei over the water.jpg|coordinates={{Coord|34.680078|N|135.781161|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|map_type=Japan|location={{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Suinin|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}}} {{GeoGroup}} {{panoViewer|Horai-yama Kofun, Haisho 360-degree.jpg|Panorama of the {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} at the Sugawara Fushimi Eastern Mausoleum of Emperor Suinin}}'''{{ill|Hōraiyama Kofun|en:draft|H%C5%8Draiyama_Kofun|simple|User:Immanuelle/H%C5%8Draiyama_Kofun|fr|User:Immanuelle/H%C5%8Draiyama%20Kofun|ja|宝来山古墳|1=Hōraiyama Kofun|tt=1185665230|2023-11-18 06:31|3273|3639|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun|qq=}}'''[[白石太一郎]] 『古墳からみた倭国の形成と展開（日本歴史 私の最新講義）』 敬文社、2013rd year 、pp. 188-199。 (宝来山古墳) (also read as Hōraisan Kofun {{Sfn|宝来山古墳（古代史）|2006}}) is a [[kofun]] located in Amagatsuji-cho (Amagatsuji-cho), {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} Although it is unclear who was actually buried there, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}} of the 11th {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin,|1=Emperor Suinin}} under the name " {{nihongo|Sugawara no Fushimi no Higashi no Misasagi|菅原伏見東陵}}." It is the 20th largest kofun in Japan , and recent surveys have estimated it to have been built around the mid- {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} (early [[Kofun period]] ). == Overview == It is a huge keyhole-shaped tumulus constructed in the northern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}, west of Nara city. To the northeast, the construction of the {{ill|10=ja|11=佐紀盾列古墳群|Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (Saki Shield-row Tomb Group) is known, consisting of large keyhole-shaped tombs such as the {{ill|10=ja|11=五社神古墳|Gosashi Kofun|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|10=ja|11=佐紀陵山古墳|Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, but there is a theory that the Horaiyama Tomb and the surrounding small circular tombs should also be included in this group as the southern branch of the Saki Tomb Group. {{Sfn|宝来山古墳（古代史）|2006}} {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} According to historical documents, the tumulus was once known as {{Nihongo|Hōraiyama|蓬莱山}} {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（平凡社）|1981}} and it is known that grave robbery occurred in 1849 {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} . Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as an imperial tomb, so no full-scale investigations have been conducted to date. The shape of the mound is circular with a keyhole, with the front part facing south. The mound is built in three tiers. {{Sfn|宝来山古墳（古代史）|2006}} {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} The length of the mound measures 227 meters (some say 240 meters), making it the 20th largest in Japan. {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and haniwa clay figures are known to exist on the outside of the mound. {{Sfn|垂仁天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} A keyhole-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and the total length of the tomb including the moat is 330 meters. {{Sfn|垂仁天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} The southeastern part of the moat was later expanded, but part of the outer bank remained and remains a small island today. {{Sfn|垂仁天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} According to historical documents documenting tomb raids during the Edo period, the burial site was a {{ill|10=ja|11=竪穴式石室|vertical stone chamber|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} containing a chest-shaped sarcophagus . {{Sfn|宝来山古墳（大和前方後円墳集成）|2001}} {{Sfn|宝来山古墳（古代史）|2006}} {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} Excavated items include {{ill|10=ja|11=円筒埴輪|Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and {{ill|Representational Haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}} (shield-, house-, and quiver-shaped haniwa) collected by the Imperial Household Agency. {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} Based on clay figures collected by the Imperial Household Agency, the Horaiyama Tomb is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, during the early [[Kofun period]] . Among the large ancient tombs in the northern Nara Basin, it is Ranked in the order of construction, following the {{ill|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (said to be the tomb of {{ill|10=ja|11=日葉酢媛命|Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}) and preceding the {{ill|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|1=Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ishizukayama Kofun|qq=}} (said to be the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}) and the {{ill|10=ja|11=五社神古墳|Gosashi Kofun|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (said to be the tomb of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingu|1=Empress Jingū}}) [[白石太一郎]] 『古墳からみた倭国の形成と展開（日本歴史 私の最新講義）』 敬文社、2013rd year 、pp. 188-199。 . In particular, the Horaiyama Tomb is considered to be the tomb of a king of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} and, unlike previous kofun, is noteworthy for being one of the earliest examples of a moat with a constant water level encircling the mound. [[広瀬和雄]] 『前方後円墳の世界（岩波新書1264）』 岩波書店、2010th year 、p. 139。 {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} Although the identity of the person buried there is unknown, the Imperial Household Agency has designated it as the tomb of the 11th Emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} . {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（平凡社）|1981}} The site of the Horaiyama Tomb was located within the Ukyo capital of [[Heijō-kyō|Heijo]]-kyo, which was built later. {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} == History of the ruins == * {{ill|1849|en|1849|qid=Q7650|lt=In 1849|1=1849}} {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|qid=Q1146125|lt=,|1=Kaei}} tomb robbery occurred. {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyu|1=Bunkyū}} 3-4 {{ill|1864|en|1864|qid=Q7704|lt=(1863-1864|1=1864}} {{ill|1863|en|1863|qid=Q7701|lt=)|1=1863}}, Bunkyu Restoration {{Sfn|奈良市史 考古編|1971}} . * During {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=the Meiji|1=Meiji era}} period, it was designated as the tomb of Emperor Suinin by the Imperial Household Ministry (now {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=the Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} ). * {{ill|1897|en|1897|qid=Q7847|lt=In 1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), the worship hall, stone fence, and iron door were remodeled. {{Sfn|奈良市史 考古編|1971}} * {{ill|1968|en|1968|qid=Q2644|lt=In 1968|1=1968}}, repairs were made to the mound’s breakwaters. {{Sfn|奈良市史 考古編|1971}} == Kofun == [[File:750314_Houraisan_Kofun_aerial.jpg|thumb|220x220px| Aerial photograph of Horaiyama Tomb (1975)
Created based on aerial photographs from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan ]] [[File:Horai-yama Kofun, zenkei over a rice paddy.jpg|thumb|220x220px|{{Center|The Kofun seen from the east (the rear round part is on the right)}}]] The size of the mound is as follows: {{Sfn|垂仁天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989}} * Total length of the tumulus: 330 meters - including the surrounding moat. * Mound length: 227 meters * Rear mound - Built in three tiers. ''' Diameter: 123 meters ''' Height: 17.3 meters * Front section - constructed in three stages. ''' Width: 118 meters ''' Height: 15.6 meters The mound is surrounded by a keyhole-shaped moat, one of the earliest examples of a moat on a water level. {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} However, while the moat in historical documents from the Edo period is depicted as symmetrical, the southeastern part of the front part of the moat as it exists today bulges outward, which is thought to be due to later expansion for irrigation purposes. {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} The small island floating within the moat (today's Hoonai Baizuka) is thought to be part of the original outer moat embankment. {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} In addition, it is believed that the current water level in the moat is higher than it was at the time of construction, leading some to speculate that the original length of the mound was around 240 meters. [[白石太一郎]] 『古墳からみた倭国の形成と展開（日本歴史 私の最新講義）』 敬文社、2013rd year 、pp. 188-199。 == Construction date == Compared to nearby large ancient tombs, there is little information available to determine its age, and little is known beyond the fact that it was roughly constructed in the mid-4th century. However, fragments of haniwa collected during the construction work carried out since the 2000s showed many characteristics consistent with the early period of Period II (early to mid-4th century), which allowed the date to be rapidly narrowed down 奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報　平成27（2015）年度、村瀨陸、2018th year 、p.94-101 . In addition, it was previously thought to have been built later than the Shibuya Mukaiyama Tomb, but excavations at the nearby Sugawara Higashi Site unearthed pottery from the latter half of Furu 1 type to Furu 2 type, which are in a style dating to the first half of the 4th century, raising the possibility that this tomb was actually built earlier 奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報　平成 30（2018）年度、村瀨陸、2021st year 、p.111-114。 . == Deceased ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+names of the tombs of Emperor Suinin and Emperor Anko ! ! {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}
(11th) ! {{ill|Emperor Ankō|en|Emperor Ankō|qid=Q361010|lt=Emperor Anko|1=Emperor Ankō}}
(20th) |- ! |[[File:Emperor_Suinin.jpg|link=File:Emperor_Suinin.jpg|112x112px]] |[[File:Emperor_Ankō.jpg|link=File:Emperor_Ankō.jpg|115x115px]] |}
It is unclear who was actually buried in the Horaiyama Tomb, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the tomb of the 11th '''{{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor, Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}''' . [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。 宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。 {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（国史）}} 『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、学生社、2014th year 、pp. 400-401。 Regarding Emperor Suinin's tomb, the Kojiki The [[Kojiki]] {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} section states that it was located in "Sugawara no Mitachi no Naka," while [[Nihon Shoki|the Nihon Shoki]] [[Nihon Shoki]], December 10th of the Lunar calendar, 99th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. states that it was located in "Sugawara no Fushimi no Mausoleum." {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（国史）}} Additionally, the Shoku [[Shoku Nihongi|Nihongi]], in its entry for the first year of the Reiki era ( {{ill|715|en|715|qid=Q23528|lt=715|1=715}} ) 『続日本紀』霊亀元年（715th year ）四月庚申（9日）条。, states that three households were assigned to guard the tomb{{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（国史）}} . In the [[Engishiki]], {{ill|10=ja|11=諸陵式|Shoryō-ryō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} 『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。, it is described as a distant tomb called "Sugawara Fushimi Eastern Tomb." Its area was 2 cho (6.8 m2) from east to west and 2 cho (6.8 m2) from north to south, and 2 cho (1.2 m2) of land was allocated to the tomb and 3 cho (1.2 m2) of land for the guardian each year. {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（国史）}} In addition to the above, the Nihon Ryōiki Volume II, Chapter 15 of '{{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konjaku Monogatarishū|qq=}}' titled 'The Novice Monk Who Was Struck for Begging and Thus Met an Evil Death as Immediate Retribution records that Inukai no Sukune Shinro lived in "the village of Saki, north of the tomb of Ikume in the capital of Nara" {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（平凡社）|1981|qq=draft}} 『新編日本古典文学全集 10 日本霊異記』小学館、2004th year （ジャパンナレッジ版）、p. 284。, and the miscellaneous chapter of the {{ill|Tōdai-ji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji Yōroku|1=Tōdaiji Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11526470}} describes his residence as "the tomb of Sugawara no Fushiminoyama" {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（国史）}} . Later, during the Genroku Imperial Investigations of Tombs in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the Nara Magistrate's Office reported that this tumulus was the tomb of Emperor Suinin (although there was once a theory that it was the tomb of {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} {{ill|10=ja|11=新田部親王|Niitabe-shinnō|lt=Nittabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Niitabe-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503777}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ), and this is still the case today. {{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（国史）}} {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} However, while the tombs of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} (the 10th emperor) and {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th emperor) are located in the southeastern part of the Nara Basin, which is said to be the birthplace of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}}, it is unnatural that Emperor Suinin's tomb is located in the northern part of the Nara Basin. The archaeological construction order also differs, so there is a theory that errors had already occurred in the tradition of Emperor Suinin's tomb by the time of the Kojiki, Nihon Shoki, and Engishiki [[森浩一]] 『天皇陵古墳への招待（筑摩選書23）』 筑摩書房、2011年、pp. 72-84, 93-94。 . According to this theory, the reason this tumulus is assumed to be the tomb of Emperor Suinin is because of the relationship between the {{ill|Haji clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji clan|qq=}}, whose main family was located near the Horaiyama Kofun, and the legend of Emperor Suinin's haniwa clay figurines. In addition, both the front and rear mounds of the tumulus are built in three tiers, which makes it possible to assume that the person buried there was a member of the royal family who was a central figure in the royal authority. There are no other Kofuns of a similar size in the surrounding area. Due to three points related to the legend of the Haji clan, there is a view that it is highly likely to be the tomb of the head of the Yamato government, who was later known to later generations as "Emperor Suinin" 荊木美行「『日本書紀』と伊勢の神宮」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、278-283頁。（原論文:『瑞垣』第248号、神宮司庁、2020th year ） . In addition, in the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the tombs of Emperor Suinin and {{ill|Emperor Ankō|en|Emperor Ankō|qid=Q361010|lt=Emperor Anko|1=Emperor Ankō}} (the 20th emperor) are listed with similar names, and there is a theory that Emperor Anko's tomb is not the current tomb (archaeologically, it may be the remains of a medieval clan residence [[森浩一]] 『天皇陵古墳への招待（筑摩選書23）』 筑摩書房、2011年、pp. 72-84, 93-94。 ) but the Hyōgoyama Tomb (currently the enclave of the tomb of Emperor Suinin; see below) near the Horaiyama Tomb {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014|qq=draft}} . == Sub-Kofun == {{GeoGroup}}It is unclear whether this tumulus had a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|lt=Secondary Tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}}, but it is assumed that there was no archaeological association with it. {{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}}[[File:Tajimamorinomikotonotuka.jpg|right|thumb|220x220px|{{Center|Kōnai Satellite Tomb (traditionally believed to be the grave of {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|qid=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori|1=Tajimamori}})}}]] [[File:Horai-yama_Kofun,_baicho-1.jpg|right|thumb|220x220px|{{Center|Exclave No. 1 (Hyōgo Mountain Kofun)}}]]There are other tombs nearby including the believed grave of {{ill|Tajimamori|en|Tajimamori|qid=Q11316133|lt=Tajimamori|1=Tajimamori}} on a small island.{{Sfn|今尾文昭|2014}} Currently, a place of worship is set up on the opposite shore of the small island .{{Sfn|菅原伏見東陵（国史）}}{{panoViewer|Horai-yama Kofun, Tajimamorinomikotonotuka 360-degree.jpg|360-degree panoramic image of the Kofun (said to be the tomb of Tajimamori) within the outer moat.}} ==Notes== ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|宝来山古墳|version=103402883|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:宝来山古墳]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Hōraiyama Kofun]] }} {{Commons category|Hōraiyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11452574}} [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
